note: Pure feasibility/measurement pilot for a user-proposed depth-based+MPC-ON search mode. No adoption decision; full design (time management, depth selection, UX) deferred to a Codex design consult (planned 2026-07-26).

engineChange.addition: --enable-mpc flag: when present, builds SearchPolicy{enable_history:true, enable_aspiration:true, enable_mpc:true} and calls the existing public API search_with_eval_with_policy (precedent: calibrate_mpc.rs's `gate` subcommand) instead of the two-branch match. Errors out if mpc_enabled feature is not compiled in. JSON output gains an `mpcStats` field only when --enable-mpc is passed.
engineChange.existingPathUnchanged: When --enable-mpc is absent (default), the exact original two-branch match code path runs unmodified, and the JSON output is unchanged.

additions: ensure_engine_built(enable_mpc: bool = False): adds --features mpc_enabled to the cargo build command when true
engine_best(..., enable_mpc: bool = False): appends --enable-mpc to the eval_cli invocation when true
play_game(..., engine_enable_mpc: bool = False): threads the flag to engine_best calls; copies mpcStats into engine_telemetry when present
harnessChange.existingPathUnchanged: Default (flag absent) behavior and output schema are byte-identical to before this change.

existingPathInvarianceProof.method: git stash the T175 diff; cargo build --release -p engine --bin eval_cli (pre-change); run `eval_cli best` (no --enable-mpc) on 2 fixed positions (initial position + 1 midgame position), save stdout. git stash pop; rebuild; re-run identical commands. Compare outputs excluding elapsedMs/nps (this codebase's own vs_edax.py#calibration_identity() already defines these as wall-clock-dependent and excludes them from equality checks).
existingPathInvarianceProof.result: All non-timing fields identical (hash match) across: (a) pre-change vs post-change without the feature, and (b) post-change without the feature vs post-change WITH the mpc_enabled feature compiled in but --enable-mpc not passed. Confirmed on both positions.

protocol.note: Identical to T158d/T162/T166/T169/T174 except engine_max_nodes (160000 -> 100000000), engine_time_ms (1500 -> 15000), and engine_enable_mpc (false -> true). Edax level differs per arm (P1=12, P2=10), matching T174/T169 respectively for paired comparison.

p2.allowDirtyReason: worktree dirty only from an in-progress tasks/T175-depth-based-mpc-pilot.md work-log edit (documentation only)

statistics.bootstrapArrayOrderingNote: Opening-level arrays ordered by opening number ascending (primary-01..primary-30). Game-level arrays ordered by (opening number ascending, then black before white). Identical convention to T158d/T162/T166/T169/T174.
statistics.note: Opening-level sign tests are not significant (p=0.136 for both, coincidentally identical improved/worsened/tied counts on independently different diff arrays -- verified not a bug). Game-level sign tests ARE significant (p<0.05) for both arms, due to larger sample size (n=60 vs n=30). Bootstrap CIs are entirely (P1) or marginally (P2, lower bound 0.10) above zero at the opening level. This nuance is reported as-is without over-interpretation; no verdict is rendered.

timeAndNodeDistribution.wallInsuranceDefinition: timedOut == true AND nodeLimitHit == false, identical to vs_edax.py's policy_calibration wallInsuranceFired definition (also used in T158d/T162/T166/T169/T174)
timeAndNodeDistribution.nodeLimitConclusion: The 100,000,000 node cap was never reached in either arm (0/1491, 0/1496): it functioned as effectively unlimited as designed.
timeAndNodeDistribution.wallInsuranceConclusion: Wall insurance fired exactly once across 2987 total budgeted-phase decisions (0.03%); the 15000ms budget was almost always sufficient for depth-12 search to complete.

mpcStatsAggregate.conclusion: MPC engaged substantially in both arms, cutting roughly 72% of eligible nodes.

anomalyCheck.mpcPathDeterminismCheck: verified separately (not via the harness's built-in non-MPC regression checks): same position run twice with --enable-mpc produced byte-identical output including mpcStats
anomalyCheck.earlyTerminationNote: 1 game in each arm ended with 63 discs (not 64) due to legitimate complete elimination of one side (same pattern as T162/T166/T169), not an anomaly: P1 primary-16/black, P2 primary-07/black

depthBasedFeasibilityObservations.strength: Switching to depth-based+MPC-ON improved mean margin by ~3.25-3.27 discs/game against both lv12 and lv10 (lv12: -6.07 -> -2.82; lv10: -2.22 -> +1.05, crossing into positive territory on average). Opening-level bootstrap CI is entirely above zero for P1 and marginally above zero for P2 (lower bound +0.10). Opening-level sign tests are not significant (p=0.136); game-level sign tests are significant (p<0.05). Larger-sample confirmation would be needed to establish robustness.
depthBasedFeasibilityObservations.timeFeasibility: Depth-12 search completed within the 15000ms budget in 2986 of 2987 budgeted-phase decisions (99.97%) across both arms; wall insurance fired only once. Mean game duration ~50 seconds, and 60 games completed in ~50 minutes per arm, which is practical.
depthBasedFeasibilityObservations.nodeVariance: Budgeted-phase node consumption is highly position-dependent: p50 ~650-700K but p90 exceeds 60M (nearly two orders of magnitude spread). This variance is relevant to future time-management design (Codex consult 2026-07-26) but was not analyzed further here (out of scope).
depthBasedFeasibilityObservations.fairComparisonCaveat: This pilot did not measure Edax's own wall-clock time consumption at level 10/12, so a truly symmetric 'equal time budget vs Edax' comparison was not established -- only that our own engine's fixed 15000ms/move budget was almost always sufficient for depth 12.

verdict: none (pure feasibility/measurement pilot, no adoption decision)
outOfScope: full time-management design (deferred to 2026-07-26 Codex design consult)
